applicability_conditions: A proof directory has a fixed list of public-safe artifacts.
At least one pond-backed consult response is recorded with runtime lineage.
A repeat request references a learning identifier but does not paste the original solution.
Replay success can be audited with explicit reconstruction questions and bounded verdict rules.
contradiction_or_boundary_rules: A repeat answer that copies the first-pass solution is not replay evidence.
Runtime recall is advisory evidence, not project-state authority.
A final verdict must stay scoped to the named proof and must not make system-wide claims.
Missing source refs or runtime hashes downgrade reconstruction to unresolved or invalid.
failure_modes: The learning record has no source refs, so replay cannot establish lineage.
The repeat request includes full original-solution text, so separation fails.
The response lacks pond-backed runtime hashes, so lineage preservation fails.
The audit uses narrative judgment without required reconstruction answers.
The final verdict claims more than the artifact bundle supports.
minimal_replay_test: Create a changed-surface repeat validator task.
Ask Inside Voice to replay learning_id proof_015_learning_001 without including the full original solution.
Require recalled refs, boundary warnings, a recommended solution structure, and replay hashes.
Build the repeat solution from the recalled three-layer pattern.
Score reconstruction questions and audit dimensions before issuing a verdict.
new_pattern: Three-layer replay completeness validation: first require pond-backed lineage hashes, then enforce first-pass/repeat separation, then require reconstruction answers plus scoped verdict and overclaim screening.
